microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's recovery validation program from a **2-person operation** into a platformized system, standardizing workflows to sustain 45+ annual resilience drills and drive **$14M+ business impact** across enterprise customers including Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp.

Led **0→1 development** of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining product requirements and self-service scheduling capabilities that reduced operational toil by 30% and transitioned execution to a scalable model.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability, delivering a rack-level drill program in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate**, surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a new continuous validation model.
Directed 14 cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility, including serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a **$1.5B+ enterprise contract**.
Built an internal AI agent and restructured planning workflows to reduce planning cycle time by **39%** and increase drill capacity by 21%, scaling team leverage through intelligent automation.
microsoft_2023: Championed **product adoption** for AI-driven code generation workflows, conducting user demos and training sessions that drove utilization across 14 key teams and saved 37 engineering hours monthly.
Influenced the product roadmap to include intent-based YAML generation by facilitating **11+ user interviews** with Azure service teams to surface critical feature gaps and unmet needs.
Improved data integrity and retrieval by implementing rigorous metadata standards and migrating documentation to an AI-powered semantic search tool, **cutting lookup time by 83%**.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched regions 28% faster by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework across 140+ teams.
Conducted discovery with 20+ service teams to identify **81 hours of manual effort** per region launch, then built a roadmap to automate critical paths and reduce operational toil at scale.
Engineered a **Power BI dashboard** tracking operational toil across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to pinpoint automation gaps and prioritize the highest-impact support investments.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by defining the legacy OS migration strategy and mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations with precision.
Facilitated Agile ceremonies including sprint planning and retrospectives, prioritizing the backlog to resolve **40+ high-priority tickets** and stabilize the developer intake process.
Drove cross-functional alignment between IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement automated CI/CD pipelines, **accelerating software deployment cycles by 25%**.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by managing the end-to-end scoping and invoicing lifecycle for 200+ monthly proposals, building strong client relationships through a streamlined CRM process.
Reduced Customer Acquisition Cost by **13%** and boosted conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing the website and Google profile for discoverability.
Improved leadership pricing models by conducting profitability analysis across **1,000+ project records** to identify and close margin leaks, directly informing strategic pricing decisions.

tailoring_notes: 'world-class experience' and 'feedback tracking' from the JD woven into pro_painters bullet 1 and microsoft_2023 bullet 1 — framing CRM and adoption work as client relationship-building and continuous improvement.
'troubleshoot' and 'root of any issue' vocabulary mirrored in amazon_robotics bullet 2 — backlog resolution and intake stabilization reframed as structured troubleshooting under hectic circumstances.
'complete satisfaction' and 'creative solutions' from the JD surfaced in pro_painters bullets 1–2 — invoicing lifecycle and GTM work reframed around resolving client friction and driving outcomes.

'feedback and action' loop from the JD reflected in microsoft_2022 bullet 2 and microsoft_2023 bullet 2 — discovery interviews and dashboard work positioned as gathering, tracking, and acting on user feedback to improve the platform experience.
